properties.rationale: K-means used to find optimal zone centroids before Voronoi tessellation, ensuring zones reflect actual business density rather than arbitrary grid splits.
Voronoi used after k-means to guarantee non-overlapping polygon zones, avoiding double-counting candidates and producing clean colonia boundaries.
gmaps_brecha = (gmaps_total - denue_count) / gmaps_total is the key informal economy proxy: high value means many visible businesses not in DENUE registry, indicating informal activity.

properties.description: OpenStreetMap screenshot showing a heatmap over the Yucatan peninsula, centered on Merida with a hot red/orange core surrounded by green/blue gradient. Label '56011' visible indicating ~56,011 DENUE businesses. Small yellow marker east of Merida.
properties.insight: Confirms extreme geographic concentration of formal businesses in Merida relative to the rest of Yucatan.

properties.description: Browser screenshot of mapa_formalizacion_merida.html showing the static map app UI: left panel with coordinate query inputs and layer checkboxes (Mapa de calor, Zonas prioritarias, Negocios DENUE), legend with 4 potential levels (Muy alto/Alto/Medio/Bajo), and RESUMEN CIUDAD stats: 1,134 zonas analizadas, 452 zonas prioritarias, 41.9% score promedio, 77.2% score maximo, 56,011 negocios DENUE. Map area is dark/blank suggesting the interactive data was not rendered.
properties.insight: Captured April 27 2026. Demonstrates the full static map pipeline was operational with 56,011 DENUE businesses and 1,134 analyzed zones.

properties.description: Architecture diagram titled 'ENCODER ResNet50 (pesos ImageNet)'. Shows a U-Net style encoder-decoder: encoder path (Input 512x512x3 RGB → conv1_relu 256x256x64 → conv2_block3 128x128x256 → conv3_block4 64x64x512 → conv4_block6 32x32x1024 → Bottleneck conv5_klock3 16x15c2048 Dropout 0.5), decoder path (ConvT+Skip 32=22x122 Dropout 0.3 → ConvT+Skip 64x64x512 Dropout 0.3 → more decoder blocks → Conv 1x1 + Sigmoid → output 512x512x1 probability per pixel). Skip connections shown as arcs from encoder to decoder (concatenation).
properties.insight: Semantic segmentation architecture used for pixel-level probability prediction, likely for informal zone mapping from satellite/aerial imagery.

properties.description: Identical to data/uploads/20260528_025351_figura1.1.jpg — duplicate upload of the ResNet50 U-Net architecture diagram.

Marketing banner for 'KLAUTEX — Startup Tecnológica · Mérida, Yucatán'. Tagline: 'Tecnología que transforma tu negocio. Soluciones de Software, Cloud, AI y Ciberseguridad.' Services listed: Web/Full Stack, Cloud/AWS, AI/ML, Cybersecurity, Eventon. Logos of React, Node, Python, AWS, Docker, and a red logo visible. Dragon/lizard mascot watermark on left.
properties.insight: Klautex is the tech startup in Merida, Yucatan behind or associated with this project. Services (AI/ML, Cloud/AWS) align with the project's tech stack.

properties.rationale: Google Maps includes informal businesses not registered in DENUE. The gap (gmaps_total - denue_total) / gmaps_total is a direct proxy for informal economy density per zone. This is the core insight of the project.

nodes.label: OSM Colonia Naming for Voronoi Zones

properties.rationale: After generating Voronoi zones, the script queries OSM Overpass API for neighbourhood/suburb names and assigns the closest OSM colonia name to each zone, falling back to 'Zona N' if no match.

nodes.norm_label: osm colonia naming for voronoi zones

nodes.label: SEPOMEX Postal Code to Colonia Enrichment
nodes.norm_label: sepomex postal code to colonia enrichment

properties.description: U-Net architecture with ResNet50 ImageNet-pretrained encoder for pixel-wise segmentation. Output is a 512x512x1 probability map per pixel.

Architectural diagram of a U-Net-style model using ResNet50 as encoder (ImageNet pretrained weights). Shows encoder stages: Input 512x512x3 RGB -> conv1_relu 256x256x64 -> conv2_block3 128x128x256 -> conv3_block4 64x64x512 -> conv4_block6 32x32x1024 -> Bottleneck conv5_klock3 16>15c2048 Dropout 0.5. Decoder stages with skip connections (concatenation): ConvT+Skip 32=22x122 Dropout 0.3 -> ConvT+Skip 64x64x512 Dropout 0.3 -> more decoder blocks -> Conv 1x1 + Sigmoid -> Output 512x512x1 (pixel-level probability map). Orange arcs illustrate skip connections from encoder to decoder.
Identical architectural diagram to reporte_1176ca4f. ResNet50 encoder with ImageNet pretrained weights, decoder with skip connections. Input 512x512x3, output 512x512x1 sigmoid probability map.
Same ResNet50 encoder-decoder diagram uploaded as part of visit KnEpCf4W on 2026-05-28. Shows encoder with skip connections to decoder. Input 512x512x3 RGB, output 512x512x1 pixel probability.

nodes.label: Copyright & Derechos de Autor Mind Map

properties.description: Mind map diagram titled 'COPYRIGHT & DERECHOS DE AUTOR'. Central node: Copyright y Derechos de Autor. Six branches: (1) Conceptos Fundamentales (Obra original, Sin registro previo, Vida del autor + 70 años); (2) Tipos de Obras Protegidas (Literarias, Musicales, Audiovisuales, Software); (3) Tipos de Derechos (Morales inalienables, Patrimoniales económicos); (4) Marco Legislativo (Convenio de Berna 1886, OMPI/WIPO, LFDA México/INDAUTOR); (5) Limitaciones y Excepciones (Uso educativo, Dominio público, Parodia); (6) Era Digital y Nuevos Retos (Piratería online, IA Generativa, Creative Commons, DRM).
properties.subject: copyright and intellectual property law

nodes.norm_label: copyright & derechos de autor mind map

nodes.label: Lorenz Curve — Digital Visibility of Barberias in Merida

properties.description: Chart titled 'Curva de Lorenz — Visibilidad Digital de Barberias en Merida. Coeficiente de Gini = 0.741'. X-axis: Proporcion acumulada de barberias (ordered lowest to highest visibility). Y-axis: Proporcion acumulada de resenas. Red curve = Lorenz Curve; dashed black = perfect equality (Gini=0); pink shaded area = inequality area. Key annotations: '80% de barberias tiene solo el 23.6% de las resenas' and 'El 10% superior concentra el 61.2% de las resenas'. Demonstrates high inequality in review distribution among barbershops in Merida.
properties.insight: Top 10% of barbershops hold 61.2% of all reviews; bottom 80% hold only 23.6%

nodes.norm_label: lorenz curve — digital visibility of barberias in merida

properties.description: Promotional mobile screen or poster for 'Feria Universitaria — iMET en tu preparatoria'. Shows mascot character (anthropomorphic iguana with headphones, giving thumbs up). nodes.label: ROC Curve — Prediccion de Formalizacion Merida Yucatan

properties.description: ROC curve chart titled 'Curva ROC — Predicción de Formalización, Mérida, Yucatán'. Compares two classifiers: RandomForest (AUC=0.718, blue) and XGBoost (AUC=0.717, red/orange), versus random baseline (AUC=0.5, dashed). Both models perform nearly identically, both well above chance. Metric: predicting business formalization in Merida, Yucatan. X-axis: Tasa Falsos Positivos; Y-axis: Tasa Verdaderos Positivos.

nodes.norm_label: roc curve — prediccion de formalizacion merida yucatan

properties.description: Horizontal bar chart titled '¿Qué variables predicen mejor la formalización? (Random Forest)'. Top features by importance: pct_recientes_1a (0.333), total_negocios (0.225), año_alta_promedio (0.154), num_sectores_distintos (0.154), pct_recientes_3a (0.133). Zero importance: pct_sector_informal (0.000), pct_micro (0.000), empleados_promedio (0.000). Shows that recent business registration rate (last year) is the strongest predictor of formalization.

ResNet50 backbone used as encoder in a U-Net style segmentation architecture. Pretrained on ImageNet. Extracts multi-scale feature maps at resolutions 256x256x64, 128x128x256, 64x64x512, 32x32x1024, 16x15x2048.
properties.rationale: Using ImageNet pretrained weights transfers learned visual features to the new segmentation task, reducing training data requirements and improving generalization.

properties.description: Skip connections from encoder feature maps concatenated into corresponding decoder layers, preserving spatial detail lost through pooling. Core component of U-Net architecture.
properties.rationale: Skip connections allow the decoder to recover fine-grained spatial information that is lost during downsampling, improving segmentation boundary accuracy.

properties.description: Decoder using ConvTranspose + Skip connection blocks with Dropout regularization (0.3 in upper blocks, 0.5 in bottleneck). Final Conv 1x1 + Sigmoid outputs per-pixel probability map 512x512x1.

nodes.label: Gini Coefficient for Digital Visibility Inequality

properties.description: Gini coefficient (0.741) measuring inequality in distribution of Google reviews among barbershops in Merida. High value indicates severe concentration of online visibility in a small fraction of businesses.
properties.rationale: Applying Gini/Lorenz analysis to business review data reveals structural inequality in digital presence — the majority of informal businesses have near-zero online visibility, making review count a proxy for formalization potential.

nodes.norm_label: gini coefficient for digital visibility inequality

properties.description: ML classification task: predict whether an informal business in Merida, Yucatan will formalize. Two models evaluated: RandomForest (AUC 0.718) and XGBoost (AUC 0.717). Both achieve similar performance well above random baseline.
properties.rationale: Predicting formalization allows government/NGO agents to prioritize outreach to businesses most likely to formalize, maximizing the impact of limited intervention resources.

properties.description: The most important feature in the Random Forest formalization prediction model. Represents the percentage of businesses in a zone that registered recently (within the past year). Importance: 0.333.
properties.rationale: A neighborhood with high recent registration rates signals a cultural or economic environment favorable to formalization — new businesses in that area are more likely to follow formal registration norms.

properties.description: Core copyright concepts: original work required, no prior registration needed, protection lasts author's life + 70 years. Two types of rights: moral (inalienable) and patrimonial (economic). Governed by Convenio de Berna (1886), OMPI/WIPO, and LFDA México/INDAUTOR.

nodes.label: Field Visit Record with Photo Evidence

properties.description: The project captures field visit records (visitas) with timestamps and photo uploads. Visits 5T4OODTL and KnEpCf4W show patterns of portrait photos (likely business owner identification) and blank images (possibly failed captures), timestamped across 2026-05-28 to 2026-06-05.

nodes.norm_label: field visit record with photo evidence
